title: sida calopoda
intro: Sida calopoda, also known as the hairy fanpetals plant, is a species of flowering plant in the mallow family Malvaceae. It is native to tropical regions of Central and South America and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, such as treating fever, sore throat, and digestive issues, as well as its antiviral and antimicrobial properties. Sida calopoda can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its extracts have been used in various herbal remedies, although its efficacy and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
